title: epacris diosmifolia
intro: Epacris diosmifolia, also known as the white heath or mountain heath, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of New South Wales and Queensland in Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Epacris diosmifolia have been traditionally used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues, and have also been used to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
